action.reasoning: Observation: NLinearCI val 1.56 — much worse than DLinear+NLinear baseline (val 1.22, eval 0.4867). Best ckpt overwritten. Analysis: Need to restore best model. The original iter-4 model gave 0.4867. Let me reproduce that and try improvements: (1) shared (channel-mixing) linear vs channel-independent, (2) longer lookback isn't allowed (96 fixed). The known best for ETTh1 96->720 is ~0.43 with PatchTST or ~0.46 with NLinear. Try simple linear (no normalization tricks) — direct linear seq->pred per channel, channel-independent — this is essentially the 'Linear' baseline. Also try with seasonal-trend decomp. Let me try a clean simple approach: channel-independent linear with last-value normalization but with proper training (low LR, more epochs, early stop). Decision: Rebuild with NLinear shared weights (one [seq_len, pred_len] matrix shared across channels) which usually works better than per-channel on ETTh1, and train for more epochs with smaller LR.
